
<div class="full-bleed-banner-about">
  <img src="images/offshore-banner.jpg" class="about-banner">
</div>

---
title: "About me"
---


::: {.columns .about-intro}
::: {.column width="32%"}

![](images/rne-interview.jpeg){.about-photo}

:::

::: {.column width="68%"}
**Marine Policy and Advocacy Officer - SEO/BirdLife**

I am a marine ecologist working to ensure that the energy transition and other human uses of the sea are compatible with the protection of marine biodiversity.

I currently work in the **Green Transition Advocacy Unit at SEO/BirdLife**, where I focus on **policy and advocacy across maritime sectors** in Spain. My work currently centres on issues related to **offshore wind**, **fisheries**, and the broader governance of marine space, with a growing emphasis on **marine spatial planning** and the role of ecology in decision-making processes.

:::
:::

I hold a **PhD in Marine Sciences** from the **University of Barcelona**. My doctoral research examined the ecological implications of floating offshore wind development in the Mediterranean, with a particular focus on **Cap de Creus** and the **Gulf of Roses**. This work explored how renewable energy infrastructure may affect vulnerable species, commercial fisheries, and marine habitats in the context of the climate and biodiversity crises.

Before that, I worked in technical and research roles at the **University of Girona** and **CEAB-CSIC**, where I studied littoral reef health and the impacts of climate change on marine ecosystems.

My background combines marine ecology, environmental assessment, and science-informed advocacy. I am particularly interested in the **science-policy interface**, **marine spatial planning**, and the ways in which ecological knowledge can contribute to more coherent and responsible governance of maritime activities.

I have also received training in **Science Diplomacy** and **Maritime Spatial Planning**, and I take part in policy and stakeholder processes related to the planning and management of marine space.

## Areas of interest

- Marine ecology  
- Offshore wind and marine biodiversity  
- Fisheries and marine conservation  
- Marine spatial planning  
- Data analysis
- Environmental impact assessment  
- Science-policy interface  
- Maritime governance
